summary.intent: Audit all 9 agnostic selection components for Epic #221 closure, ensuring spec alignment, then split the monolithic selection test files into per-component modules.
summary.outcome: Test files split into per-component modules, spec drift fixed in 4 component specs (Combobox, Listbox, Menu, Select), a SegmentGroup controlled-value bug fixed, an audit record added, and a PR pushed to `codex/selection-agnostic-epic-audit`.

repo: The per-component test module split pattern is already established in data-display, input, navigation, overlay, and utility categories — selection follows the same shape: `category/mod.rs` aggregator + one module per component + shared `common.rs` for fixtures.
The selection proptest suite has an intentional 2-active / 7-ignored split; the ignored tests are not failures and must be preserved unless a concrete reason to activate them is found.
The checkout lives in a git linked worktree and was in detached HEAD state; a named branch must be created with `git switch -c` before any implementation work.
Epic #221 acceptance criteria explicitly require all 9 agnostic components: Autocomplete, Combobox, ContextMenu, Listbox, Menu, MenuBar, SegmentGroup, Select, TagsInput.
`cargo xcov` enforces a 90% branch coverage threshold on `ars-components`; the suite reached 98.5% lines / 90.0% branches after the audit changes.
ContextMenu and MenuBar specs declare their `Part` enum members in prose rather than a Rust snippet, unlike the other 7 selection component specs.

code.finding: Bug: `SetValue(Some(disabled_key))` in the controlled-prop sync path bypassed the enabled-only guard, allowing a disabled item key to enter selected state. Fix: sanitize externally synced values the same way user selection does — only present, enabled keys are accepted; invalid controlled values sync to `None`.

Spec drift: implementation exposed range and page navigation events (e.g. `SelectItemRange`, `PageDown`) not listed in the spec event enum snippet; spec was updated to match the public implementation surface.
Spec drift: implementation exposed sync/description events absent from the spec; spec event snippet updated to match.
Spec drift: implementation exposed controlled-value and collection-update events not listed in spec; spec updated.
Spec drift: implementation exposed adapter-sync events absent from spec event snippet; spec updated.

workflow: Running `cargo llvm-cov` and `cargo xcov` concurrently causes an OS SIGKILL — instrumented builds must be run sequentially, not in parallel.
A Python script using regex against both spec markdown and Rust source is an effective way to diff Event and Part enum variants across 9 component pairs without reading each file manually.
`cargo insta test -p ars-components --lib -- selection` matches more tests than expected because the filter substring also hits shared selection utilities outside the selection category module.

`cargo xci --profile fast` runs 10 sequential gates (fmt → clippy → unit → adapter → spec snippets → error coverage → mutual exclusion → snapshot count → native coverage → threshold check); the slow UI contract test suites inside the unit gate can run for 2+ minutes each.
New split files created outside git tracking do not appear in `git diff --stat`; use `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` to confirm untracked new modules are present before staging.

friction: OS killed `cargo llvm-cov` with SIGKILL when it ran concurrently with `cargo xcov`; had to re-run coverage commands sequentially.
`cargo xci --profile fast` required many polling cycles — the slow UI test suites ran past 2 minutes each, making the total gate very long.
The checkout was in detached HEAD state inside a linked worktree, requiring an explicit `git switch -c` before any implementation could begin.
Broadening proptest event strategies immediately surfaced a real SegmentGroup bug, blocking the green proptest run and requiring an unplanned implementation fix mid-audit.
`cargo xtask spec search` / `cargo xtask spec info` did not clearly return usable results; the component list had to be assembled from `spec/manifest.toml` and the epic body instead.
open_items: 7 selection proptests remain `#[ignore]`'d — activating them is future work once the machines are stable enough to generate shrinkable counterexamples.
Epic #221 is not yet closed; closure requires the PR to be merged, CI to pass, and Codex review approval.
ContextMenu and MenuBar specs still declare `Part` members in prose rather than a Rust enum snippet, inconsistent with the other 7 component specs — minor cleanup deferred.
No Leptos or Dioxus adapter implementation was included in this audit; adapter-side work for the selection category remains out of scope for this PR.
